raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus primarily on writing polish, argument/theorem consistency, or section structure and contribution framing?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section makes contribution and gap claims that may need reframing at the thesis or positioning level.","R1.2":"The delegation plausibly includes checking whether the identifiability result, finite-sample guarantee, and interpretive conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The section links theoretical claims and experimental setup to expected empirical advantages, so support for those claims is a central responsibility if content help is intended.","R1.5":"Because the request targets an entire section, subsection-level ordering and narrative arc are plausible central responsibilities.","R1.6":"A broad request for help with a paper section commonly includes sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection must disclose uncertainty caused by the underspecified delegation.","RX.2":"The projection must avoid inflating the requested responsibility beyond what the delegation supports.","RX.3":"The projection must preserve the boundary between projecting responsibilities and actually revising or evaluating the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid downstream harm from misclassifying a broad academic-writing request as a narrower or more decisive task.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus primarily on writing polish, argument/theorem consistency, or section structure and contribution framing?

rationale.R1.1: The section makes contribution and gap claims that may need reframing at the thesis or positioning level.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation plausibly includes checking whether the identifiability result, finite-sample guarantee, and interpretive conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section links theoretical claims and experimental setup to expected empirical advantages, so support for those claims is a central responsibility if content help is intended.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets an entire section, subsection-level ordering and narrative arc are plausible central responsibilities.
rationale.R1.6: A broad request for help with a paper section commonly includes sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must disclose uncertainty caused by the underspecified delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must avoid inflating the requested responsibility beyond what the delegation supports.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must preserve the boundary between projecting responsibilities and actually revising or evaluating the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid downstream harm from misclassifying a broad academic-writing request as a narrower or more decisive task.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
